title: The Truth About Sirius Black
prose: The story that eventually comes out — pieced together from professors who won't quite confirm it and students who swear they have it firsthand — turns the whole year's fear inside out. Sirius Black wasn't guilty of what he went to Azkaban for. He'd been framed, twelve years ago, by a man everyone thought was dead: Peter Pettigrew, who'd been hiding in plain sight ever since.

Professor Lupin — this year's Defence Against the Dark Arts teacher, and by every account one of the best the post has had in years — resigns before the end of term. The reason spreads almost as fast as the Sirius Black story: he's a werewolf, and once it's known, the Ministry paperwork all but writes his resignation for him.

Sirius Black is never caught. By the time the story settles, most of the school has quietly decided he's less a danger than a man who was owed twelve years he's never getting back — though the Ministry's official position remains considerably less forgiving.

choices.label: You find yourself hoping Sirius Black gets some kind of justice eventually.
You reserve judgment — it's a strange story to take entirely on faith.
